zh.Ormesby, Lincolnshire, England: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Ormesby II於1150年出生在英國林可爾史爾時，他的父親Oswald de Ormesby II45歲，母親Anastasia of Ormesby29歲。他和Mrs. Richard De Ormesby至少有1個兒子。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ormesby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Ormsby
meanings: English: habitational name from North and South Ormsby (Lincolnshire), or either of two places called Ormesby in Norfolk and the North Yorkshire. The placenames all derive from the Old Norse personal name Ormr + Old Norse bȳ ‘farmstead, village’. This surname is also common in Ireland (Connacht), where it has been established since the late 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Ormesby II was born in 1150, in Lincolnshire, England, his father, Oswald de Ormesby II, was 45 and his mother, Anastasia of Ormesby, was 29. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. Richard De Ormesby.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Ormesby II est né en 1150, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Oswald de Ormesby II, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Anastasia of Ormesby, avait 29 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. Richard De Ormesby.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Ormesby II 1150 in Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Oswald de Ormesby II 45 und seine Mutter Anastasia of Ormesby 29. Er hatte mit Mrs. Richard De Ormesby mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Ormesby II è nato il nel 1150 nel Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Oswald de Ormesby II, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Anastasia of Ormesby, aveva 29 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. Richard De Ormesby.

ja.Ormesby, Lincolnshire, England: イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Ormesby IIが1150年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Oswald de Ormesby IIは45歳，母Anastasia of Ormesbyは29歳であった。彼はMrs. Richard De Ormesbyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Ormesby II(이)가 1150년 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Oswald de Ormesby II(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Anastasia of Ormesby(은)는 29세였다. 그는 Mrs. Richard De Ormesby(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Ormesby II nasceu em 1150, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Oswald de Ormesby II, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Anastasia of Ormesby, tinha 29 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs. Richard De Ormesby.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Ormesby II родился в 1150 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Oswald de Ormesby II было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Anastasia of Ormesby, 29. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs. Richard De Ormesby.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Ormesby II nació en 1150, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Oswald de Ormesby II, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Anastasia of Ormesby, tenía 29 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs. Richard De Ormesby.
